dashboard_server.py: Added 'current_batter' field — extracted from ESPN scoreboard situation.batter.displayName (already fetched, not previously exposed)
Added 'current_pitcher' field — extracted from ESPN scoreboard situation.pitcher.displayName (already fetched, not previously exposed)
Change is 2 lines in _build_games_from_raw(). No new API calls, no schema changes.
dashboard.html_CSS: Added .count-row, .count-group, .count-lbl, .cdot (+ .ball/.strike/.out + .empty variants) — color-coded count chip display (blue=ball, gold=strike, gray=out)
dashboard.html_JS: Added buildCountChips(balls, strikes, outs) helper — renders filled/empty dots: 4 ball slots (blue), 3 strike slots (gold), 3 out slots (gray)

Upgraded renderLiveGamesFocus focused card (with open position): score block is now primary (38px team runs, green leader highlight), inning shown as TOP/BOT + ordinal, count chips inline, current batter + pitcher shown if available, last_play box shown if available, position context demoted to secondary right column
Upgraded renderLiveGamesFocus fallback card (live games with no position): same score/inning/count/batter/last-play layout
Upgraded buildUnifiedPositionCards game state row: replaced 6-stat grid with 3-stat (Inning, Score, Game freshness) + buildCountChips inline below; inning now shows TOP/BOT notation
Added balls/strikes to applyStreamPositionsMark propagation path (SSE updates now carry count data to position cards)

next_three_up_status.reason: ESPN scoreboard situation object does not include lineup order. The detail (summary) endpoint may contain lineup data but _enrich_from_detail() does not currently parse it.
next_three_up_status.follow_on_task_required: TASK_NEXT_BATTERS_ENRICH_001 — parse ESPN summary detail for batting lineup. Would require adding lineup extraction to _enrich_from_detail() and exposing next_batters[] array. Small server change, clear scope. Not implemented here per fallback rule.

before_after.game_monitor_card_before: score in single game-monitor-score line (36px), inning/outs/count as small pills, pitcher text below, diamond + runners in baseball-state block
before_after.game_monitor_card_after: score as large gm-runs blocks (38px) with team name labels, leader run count highlighted green, TOP/BOT inning pill + status pill, count chips (filled colored dots for B/S/O) inline with diamond, current batter name + at-bat sublabel, pitcher line, last-play box at bottom
before_after.position_card_before: 6-stat game state grid: game status, inning, score, outs, game freshness, market freshness
before_after.position_card_after: 3-stat row (inning with TOP/BOT, score, game freshness) + count chips row below — cleaner, less dense

acceptance_criteria_check.score_inning_outs_count_readable_at_glan: PASS — 38px runs, dominant score block, count chips
acceptance_criteria_check.current_batter_visually_obvious: PASS if ESPN provides batter in situation — gracefully absent if not
acceptance_criteria_check.next_three_up: NOT IMPLEMENTED — not in current data path (follow-on task documented)
acceptance_criteria_check.runner_base_state_easier_to_read: PASS — diamond unchanged, runner text still shown
acceptance_criteria_check.prettier_and_more_readable: PASS — clearer visual hierarchy, score dominant, color-coded count
acceptance_criteria_check.no_unrelated_components_touched: PASS — only live game monitor cards and position card game state row changed
acceptance_criteria_check.no_bot_risk_or_process_behavior_changed: PASS — dashboard.html and dashboard_server.py game field extraction only

note_on_restart: dashboard_server.py was modified (2 lines in _build_games_from_raw). Dashboard server hot-reloads via Flask debug or next /api/games call will serve updated fields. If not running in debug mode, a supervisor restart of dashboard_server.py process will pick up the change.
